question: What are the RPV height and inner diameter from Table 5.3-1, and how does Figure 5.3-1 show the vessel cross section?

nuscale_ch05_0018.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0018.summary: This section outlines the inspection, shipment, installation, and inservice surveillance requirements for the NuScale reactor vessel, referencing relevant ASME and ASTM standards. It specifies procedures for maintaining a dry, controlled environment during shipment and details the applicable codes and standards for quality assurance and material testing.

nuscale_ch05_0000.title: Preface
nuscale_ch05_0000.summary: The Preface outlines the copyright and usage restrictions for the NuScale Standard Plant Design Certification Application, specifically Chapter Five on the Reactor Coolant System and Connecting Systems. It specifies that only the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission (NRC) is authorized to use, copy, or disclose the document for regulatory purposes, and any copies must include the copyright notice. The section also references the table of contents for Chapter Five, detailing its structure and topics.

nuscale_ch05_0014.title: Reactor Vessel
nuscale_ch05_0014.summary: The Reactor Vessel section describes the NuScale Power Module's reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core, steam generators, and pressurizer within a single vessel surrounded by a containment vessel. The RPV is a critical pressure-retaining component that forms part of the reactor coolant pressure boundary, supporting various systems and ensuring the containment of fission products. Materials used for the RPV are selected according to ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code standards to ensure integrity and safety throughout the plant's design lifetime.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel throughout its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that all criteria remain within regulatory limits under anticipated operating conditions.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes key components of the NuScale reactor system. It details the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core and supports major systems, and outlines the main Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping and its connections. The section also introduces the pressurizer (PZR), which is integral to the RPV, and references further detailed descriptions in subsequent sections.

edges.description: Section A references regulatory and procedural requirements for RPV inspection and handling, while Section B provides a general description of the RPV's structure and function, with both sections contextually connected by their focus on the reactor pressure vessel as the subject of design and regulatory compliance.

Section A is a preface providing document context, while Section B introduces the reactor vessel and its components, setting the stage for where Table 5.3-1 and Figure 5.3-1 would provide specific RPV dimensions and cross-sectional details.

Section A provides a general description of the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure the RPV's integrity under specified conditions, both relating to the vessel's design and operational requirements.

Section B provides specific references to regulatory standards and requirements for the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which are relevant to the system components described in Section A, as compliance with these standards underpins the design and operational details discussed.

Section B describes the reactor pressure vessel (RPV) and references its design and function, while Section A references the RPV in the context of system operations and preparation steps, connecting them through the shared component (RPV) but not directly addressing the specific design values or figures requested in the analysis question.
